nodes.label: All data is noise — can the cheap brain learn with no clean truth?
nodes.rationale: Door B: the author's insight that the model is online/lifelong and never sees a clean curated set — is a stable class direction even learnable from an all-noisy stream? Answer: learnable for the zero-mean part, and partly for free.

nodes.label: Noise2Noise (clean recoverable from only-noisy pairs)
nodes.rationale: For zero-mean/unbiased noise, a regression loss against a noisy target converges in expectation to the same estimator as clean-target supervision; caveat — this is L2-specific and our InfoNCE isn't an L2-to-target regression, so the contrastive transfer is a hypothesis (P6.4 tests it), not a direct theorem application.

nodes.label: Self-Purified Replay (buffer purity, not just averaging)
nodes.rationale: The purity of the replay buffer is crucial — a naive buffer accumulates corrupted samples and pulls the mean off; a Self-Centered Filter keeps a pure buffer; 'forgetting can be mitigated even with noisy labels via self-supervised learning' = the project's exact SCFF+LUT+sleep loop, validated under noise. Correction to design §3.4: averaging alone is incomplete, purity must be added to the LUT design.

nodes.label: One enemy, three faces: directional/structured noise
nodes.rationale: Structured/directional (biased) noise survives auto-zero (Door A), survives replay averaging, and survives Noise2Noise (Door B) — the same hard residual appears three ways, meaning Phase 6 has one crux to beat, not three.
